Title: Reinventing metabolic pathways: Independent evolution of benzoxazinoids in flowering plants
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts indole-3-glycerol phosphate to indole, providing the entry substrate for benzoxazinoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial and antifeedant defense compound; also associated broadly with allelopathy and iron uptake functions of benzoxazinoids.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots, young leaves, and flowers in Aphelandra squarrosa; not fully developed leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports reconstituted A. squarrosa benzoxazinoid pathway leading primarily to DIMBOA-Glc accumulation in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstitution by transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with downstream AsFMO and AsBx4-7 plus metabolite profiling by LC-qTOF.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsIGL had been previously identified from A. squarrosa and was used as the entry enzyme for pathway reconstitution. 2. Coexpression with AsFMO and AsBx4-7 in N. benthamiana led to benzoxazinoid accumulation, primarily DIMBOA-Glc. 3. This supports AsIGL as the precursor-supplying BX1-like enzyme in A. squarrosa.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously identified and characterized from A. squarrosa; specific expression pattern in this paper not fully detailed.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome data; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA967136
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana greenhouse-grown plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: IGL had been identified previously rather than newly discovered in this study; included because it was functionally used in complete pathway reconstitution.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts indole-3-glycerol phosphate to indole, initiating benzoxazinoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial and antifeedant defense compound; benzoxazinoids also broadly function in allelopathy and iron uptake.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: All tested tissues in Lamium galeobdolon
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports reconstituted L. galeobdolon benzoxazinoid pathway and enables substantial DIBOA-Glc accumulation in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Complete pathway reconstitution by transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with LgFMO, LgBX4, LgBX5, and LgBX8; LC-qTOF metabolite detection.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LgIGL1 was used as the BX1-like entry enzyme in pathway reconstitution. 2. Coexpression with LgFMO, LgBX4, LgBX5, and LgBX8 yielded DIBOA-Glc accumulation in N. benthamiana. 3. LgIGL1 is likely involved in benzoxazinoid formation in L. galeobdolon.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously identified and characterized from L. galeobdolon; specific quantitative pattern not detailed here.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome data; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA967136
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana greenhouse-grown plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LgIGL1 was distinguished from LgIGL2 by higher functional support in pathway reconstitution.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts indole-3-glycerol phosphate to indole, but with weak support for contribution to benzoxazinoid production in the tested reconstruction system.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial and antifeedant defense compound; benzoxazinoids also broadly function in allelopathy and iron uptake.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: All tested tissues in Lamium galeobdolon
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Only trace DIBOA-Glc accumulation when used in pathway reconstitution, suggesting limited or no major role in benzoxazinoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana with LgFMO, LgBX4, LgBX5, and LgBX8; LC-qTOF metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LgIGL2 was tested as an alternative BX1-like enzyme in L. galeobdolon pathway reconstruction. 2. Only traces of DIBOA-Glc were detected when LgIGL2 replaced LgIGL1. 3. The results suggest that LgIGL2 is less likely than LgIGL1 to function in L. galeobdolon benzoxazinoid formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously identified and characterized from L. galeobdolon; specific quantitative pattern not detailed here.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome data; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA967136
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana greenhouse-grown plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper directly compares LgIGL1 and LgIGL2 in pathway reconstruction and infers LgIGL1, not LgIGL2, as the relevant pathway enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctional oxidation of indole to 3-hydroxyindolin-2-one, most likely via indolin-2-one as an intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial and antifeedant defense compound; benzoxazinoids also contribute to allelopathy and iron uptake.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots, young leaves, and flowers in Aphelandra squarrosa
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables production of 3HI2O-Glc when expressed with ZmBx1 or after indole/I2O feeding, establishing the BX2/BX3 equivalent activity in A. squarrosa.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with ZmBx1 or ZmBx1-2; substrate infiltration with indole or I2O; LC-qTOF metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsFMO functionally replaced the separate BX2 and BX3 oxidations of grasses. 2. AsFMO produced 3HI2O from either indole or I2O in N. benthamiana, indicating bifunctional activity. 3. AsFMO represents a distinct FMO-based catalytic solution for early benzoxazinoid biosynthesis in eudicots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected as candidate from BXD-accumulating tissues versus nonaccumulating fully developed leaves in A. squarrosa transcriptomics.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome data; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA967136
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for establishing early BXD oxidation in eudicots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Indole or I2O infiltration into transiently transformed N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Active recombinant protein could not be isolated from multiple heterologous systems, so mechanistic enzymology remained unresolved.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctional oxidation of indole to 3-hydroxyindolin-2-one, most likely via indolin-2-one as an intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial and antifeedant defense compound; benzoxazinoids also contribute to allelopathy and iron uptake.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: All tested tissues in Lamium galeobdolon
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables production of 3HI2O-Glc when expressed with ZmBx1 or after indole/I2O feeding, establishing the BX2/BX3 equivalent activity in L. galeobdolon.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with ZmBx1 or ZmBx1-2; substrate infiltration with indole or I2O; LC-qTOF metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LgFMO functionally replaced the separate BX2 and BX3 steps of grasses. 2. LgFMO converted indole or I2O to 3HI2O in planta, showing bifunctional oxidation activity. 3. This identifies an independently evolved FMO solution for early benzoxazinoid biosynthesis in L. galeobdolon.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in L. galeobdolon leaves compared with closely related nonproducing Lamium species in comparative transcriptomics.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome data; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA967136
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for establishing early BXD oxidation in eudicots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Indole or I2O infiltration into transiently transformed N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Related Lamiales FMOs from both BXD producers and nonproducers also showed BX2/BX3-like activity, suggesting widespread latent catalytic capacity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 3-hydroxyindolin-2-one to 2-hydroxy-3,4-dihydro-1,4-benzoxazin-3-one through oxidative ring expansion.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial and antifeedant defense compound; benzoxazinoids also contribute to allelopathy and iron uptake.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots, young leaves, and flowers in Aphelandra squarrosa
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces HBOA-Glc in planta and catalyzes the BX4 step in vitro, establishing the HBOA-forming step of the A. squarrosa pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with ZmBx1-3 plus ZmBx8; microsomal in vitro assay in Saccharomyces cerevisiae using synthetic 3HI2O; LC-qTOF/LC-MS analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsBX4 is a CYP92 enzyme that catalyzes conversion of 3HI2O to HBOA. 2. Activity was validated both in N. benthamiana and in yeast microsomes. 3. AsBX4 is evolutionarily unrelated to maize BX4, supporting independent recruitment in eudicots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified among candidate genes enriched in BXD-accumulating A. squarrosa tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome data; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA967136
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; yeast microsome assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP92 enzymes with BX4 activity were also found in Scoparia dulcis and Wrightia religiosa, but closely related inactive CYP92s also exist.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 3-hydroxyindolin-2-one to 2-hydroxy-3,4-dihydro-1,4-benzoxazin-3-one through oxidative ring expansion.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial and antifeedant defense compound; benzoxazinoids also contribute to allelopathy and iron uptake.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: All tested tissues in Lamium galeobdolon
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces HBOA-Glc in planta and catalyzes the BX4 step in vitro, establishing the HBOA-forming step of the L. galeobdolon pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with ZmBx1-3 plus ZmBx8; microsomal in vitro assay in Saccharomyces cerevisiae using synthetic 3HI2O; LC-qTOF/LC-MS analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LgBX4 is a CYP92 enzyme that catalyzes the conversion of 3HI2O to HBOA. 2. Activity was validated both in planta and in vitro. 3. LgBX4 is evolutionarily distinct from both maize BX4 and AsBX4, supporting independent recruitment.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in L. galeobdolon leaves compared with nonproducing Lamium species.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome data; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA967136
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; yeast microsome assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Closely related CYP92s from some Lamium species and Marrubium vulgare lacked BX4 activity despite phylogenetic proximity.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial and antifeedant defense compound; benzoxazinoids also contribute to allelopathy and iron uptake.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots, young leaves, and flowers in Aphelandra squarrosa
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Allows DIBOA-Glc production in N. benthamiana, demonstrating BX5 activity in A. squarrosa.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient functional complementation in Nicotiana benthamiana with ZmBx1-4 plus ZmBx8 and metabolite profiling by LC-qTOF.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsBX5 is a CYP81 that catalyzes HBOA N-hydroxylation to DIBOA. 2. It functionally replaced maize BX5 in N. benthamiana. 3. Recruitment from CYP81 indicates independent evolution of BX5 activity in the Acanthaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified among candidate genes enriched in BXD-accumulating A. squarrosa tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome data; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA967136
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Attempts to express AsBX5 in yeast for in vitro analysis were unsuccessful.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial and antifeedant defense compound; benzoxazinoids also contribute to allelopathy and iron uptake.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: All tested tissues in Lamium galeobdolon
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Allows DIBOA-Glc production in N. benthamiana, demonstrating BX5 activity in L. galeobdolon.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient functional complementation in Nicotiana benthamiana with ZmBx1-4 plus ZmBx8 and metabolite profiling by LC-qTOF.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LgBX5 is a CYP71 that catalyzes HBOA N-hydroxylation to DIBOA. 2. It functionally replaced maize BX5 in N. benthamiana. 3. It is distinct from both maize BX5 and AsBX5, supporting independent evolution of BX5 activity in L. galeobdolon.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in L. galeobdolon leaves; LgBx5-like showed only trace leaf expression.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome data; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA967136
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Closely related BX5-like enzymes from Lamium species lacked BX5 activity in planta despite high sequence similarity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to benzoxazinoid aglycones, accepting HBOA and DIBOA as substrates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stable glucosylated defense metabolite for storage; antimicrobial and antifeedant roles.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: All tested tissues in Lamium galeobdolon
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes glucosylation of HBOA and DIBOA, enabling accumulation of stable benzoxazinoid glucosides and completion of the L. galeobdolon pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression with maize ZmBx1-5 in Nicotiana benthamiana and in vitro enzyme assays with purified protein/substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LgBX8 is a UGT85-family glucosyltransferase involved in benzoxazinoid stabilization. 2. It glucosylated HBOA and DIBOA, but not 3HI2O, in vitro. 3. LgBX8 is unrelated to maize BX8/9, indicating independent recruitment of glucosylation function.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in L. galeobdolon leaves compared with nonproducing Lamium species.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome data; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA967136
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for stable glucoside accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; in vitro assays with purified enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No functional BX8 counterpart could be identified in A. squarrosa despite extensive screening.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial and antifeedant defense compound; methoxylated benzoxazinoid glucoside.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots, young leaves, and flowers in Aphelandra squarrosa
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Drives formation of downstream DIMBOA-Glc when coexpressed with BX7 and specifically hydroxylates DIBOA-Glc in vitro, establishing the BX6 step in A. squarrosa.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with ZmBx1-5 plus ZmBx7-8; purified recombinant enzyme assay in Escherichia coli with DIBOA-Glc and DIBOA substrates; LC-qTOF analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsBX6 is a 2-ODD acting as the BX6 enzyme in A. squarrosa. 2. It accepted DIBOA-Glc but not DIBOA in vitro, indicating glucosylation precedes this hydroxylation step. 3. AsBX6 is phylogenetically distinct from maize BX6, supporting independent evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Among the top 10 candidate genes highly expressed in young leaves and flowers of A. squarrosa.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome data; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA967136
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; in vitro assays with Fe2+, ascorbate, 2-oxoglutarate
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two A. squarrosa PPOs showed only trace BX6-like activity and were considered unlikely to act in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates TRIBOA-Glc using SAM to form DIMBOA-Glc.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial and antifeedant defense compound; methoxylated benzoxazinoid glucoside.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots, young leaves, and flowers in Aphelandra squarrosa
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes final conversion to DIMBOA-Glc in planta and in vitro, completing the A. squarrosa benzoxazinoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with upstream maize pathway genes; in vitro assay with purified protein and TRIBOA-Glc plus SAM; LC-qTOF analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsBX7 is the terminal O-methyltransferase of the A. squarrosa pathway. 2. It converted TRIBOA-Glc to DIMBOA-Glc in planta and in vitro. 3. AsBX7 is phylogenetically unrelated to maize BX7, supporting independent evolution of the terminal methylation step.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as candidate OMT from BXD-accumulating tissues of A. squarrosa.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome data; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA967136
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; in vitro assays with SAM
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper figure legend appears to contain a typo referring to AsBX6 in panel B, but the text clearly states AsBX7 was assayed with TRIBOA-Glc.
